title: Inspect repo docs/skills alignment for note-lead short-teaser policy after runtime exemption. Focus only on these files in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public: skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md, docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md, docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md. Report exact lines or wording...
summary_text: Coverage mapping for remaining x-auto residuals: - Narrow note-lead exemption - x-auto tests - [`tests/test_approval_guard.py` (x-auto)](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_approval_guard.py:9) verifies note posts without thumbnails are exempt in approval logic. - [`tests/test_approval_guard.py` (x-auto)](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_approval_guard.py:16) covers quote/RT-like exemptions in the same class. - [`tests/test_scheduler_gate1.py` (x-auto)](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_gate1.py:326) checks short note promo skips image check. - [`tests/test_scheduler_gate1.py` (x-auto)](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_gate1.py:350) checks post-cutoff short note teaser passes gate. - [`tests/test_prepare_post_bundle.py` (x-auto)](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_prepare_post_bundle.py:232) checks short note acceptance behavior in prepare flow. - repo-visible tests/docs - [`docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md) states the short note lead rule and short-teaser behavior. - [`test-x-auto-note-linking-rule.sh` (/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-x-auto-note-linking-rule.sh)](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-x-auto-note-linking-rule.sh) enforces note-linking policy via contract phrasing. - `created_at` malformed/empty handling - x-auto tests - No explicit negative tests found for missing/empty/malformed `created_at` in the inspected files. - Closest relevant medical/claim coverage does not include timestamp parsing edge cases. - repo-visible tests - No direct `created_at` malformed/empty case was found in the public `tests/` shell suite. - Gap: this is the main uncovered residual. - Clearing stale Reply URLs on normal update - x-auto tests - [`tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py` (x-auto)](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py:707) explicitly tests clearing local `reply_url` when notion source has no reply URL. - [`tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py` (x-auto)](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py:697) also covers notion-preferred URL precedence. - repo-visible tests/docs - [`docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md) and [`docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md) state stale reply/thread URL cleanup expectations. - Public shell tests do not include a runtime-level merge/update stale-reply-url assertion. Minimal regression tests to add (report-only): 1. Add malformed/empty `created_at` tests in x-auto unit layer - Add parametrized cases in [`test_scheduler_gate1.py`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_gate1.py) or a focused guard test file: - input `created_at = ""` - missing `created_at` - malformed (`2026-99-99`, `bad-date`, `2026/04/06`) - assert deterministic reject/demotion path and exact block reason. 2. Add explicit “note lead + medical/AI-diagnostic but no primary source” regression - Add one minimal fixture in [`test_prepare_post_bundle.py`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_prepare_post_bundle.py) that is short-note-form/teaser-style but contains medical or AI-diagnostic claim language and no valid `source_url`. - assert citation requirement still blocks (not treated as exempt by length logic). - Mirror in [`test_approval_guard.py`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_approval_guard.py) for decision-layer parity. 3. Decide whether to extend public repo-visible tests - If you want explicit repo-level behavior: add a minimal integration-style case to public `tests/` that asserts stale `reply_url` is not retained when notion payload lacks it. - If no harness for merge semantics exists in public shell tests, leave as doc/contract assertions plus existing x-auto unit coverage (already strong on this edge).
